meaning: adj.舞台表演区的 台上的,台上演出的
examples: They were persuaded onstage for a laugh.
When she walked onstage she was given a standing ovation.
Silent onstage communication is key, and each pianist has their own style of "nodding" to indicate a page turn which they need to practise with their page turner.
Spelling tests, both computer-based and onstage, will make up the other half of their scores.
At the forums, he said he sometimes comes up with ideas as he sits onstage.
